HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

Appearances: Mary Ann Rousso of Hemar Rousso & Heald LLP (818.501.3800) for judgment creditor LSC 906-908 Broadway LLC; Natalia Cherkashina (415.690.5182) claimant appearing in propria persona, assisted in the Russian language by court certified interpreter Tatiana Iacona.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Mrs. Cherkashina's Claim of Exemption is DENIED. The claim under Section 703.140 fails because neither judgment debtor is in bankruptcy. The claim under Section 703.530 fails because the initial claim documents did not have a "financial statement" with all of the required information. (See CCP Section 703.530(b)(1)-(5)). The Court notes that the initial submission was drastically incomplete, and evidence brought up in Reply to is an improper attempt to comply with the requirements of CCP Section 703.530(b). To the extent that the evidence in Reply, or Claimant's request to evaluate the evidence under CCP Section 704.225 in Reply, can even be considered, the Court finds that the income garnished was not "necessary for the support of the judgment debtor and the spouse and dependents of the judgment debtor." (CCP 703.530(a), 704.225). Primarily, evidence from both sides demonstrates that Defendant continues to receive significant business income. Claimant cites no authority for the idea that Mrs. Cherkashina's personal loans render the income garnished "necessary" to support the family. Claimant also failed to attach a promissory note or letter from the former business partner who deposited $200,000 into the Defendant's company's bank account, 75% of which was later transferred into Mrs. Cherkashina's savings account, calling into question whether this deposit was a "personal loan." The court will issue an order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment Pursuant To C.C.P. 585(B),(D), 1005 And S.F. Local Rule 6.6 (D-7); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-05-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendants Startup Temple Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and Pavel Cherkashin, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:56 a.m., adjourning at 11:07 a.m., and reconvening at 11:11 a.m.

Christopher S. Reeder, Esq. appeared for plaintiff LSC 906-908 Broadway LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.
Eugene Rozovsky, Senior Vice President and Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath, but did not testify.
Loan Officer Jonathan Shapiro was placed under oath, but did not testify.
Ali Kelly, Assistant General Counsel for plaintiff, was also present.
Messrs. Reeder, Rozovsky and Shapiro, along with Ms. Kelly, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Startup Temple Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation, or Pavel Cherkashin, an individual.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Specific Performance, Declaratory Relief, Judicial Foreclosure, Quiet Title, Enforcement of Guaranty, Apointment of Receiver and Injunctive Relief was filed on April 6, 2021, and personally served on defendant Startup Temple Holdings, Inc's authorized Agent of Process on April 24, 2021.
Plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents were personally served on defendant Pavel Cherkashin, a individual, on May 2, 2021.
Default as to defendant Startup Temple Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation, was entered on May 26, 2021.
Default as to defendant Pavel Cherkashin, an individual, was entered on June 3, 2021.
Order appointing Kevin Singer Receiver over the real property commonly known as 906-908 Broadway, San Francisco, CA 94115, was filed on June 29, 2021.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing filed March 8, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff LSC 906-908 Broadway LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and against the defaulted defendants Startup Temple Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and Pavel Cherkashin, an individual, as follows:

$6,543,535.25 in principal & interest as of 09/21/2021;
$594,370.46 in prejudgment interest from 09/22/2021 to 04/28/2022;
$265,987.09 in property tax advances;
$2,446.00 in insurance premium advances; and
$158,639.20 in attorney fees; and $34,141.64 in costs.

Total - $7,599,119.64.*

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 11:19 a.m.

*The total amount of $7,599,119.64 will be verified by the Court prior to signing-off on the judgment.
